title: Executive Order 9337—Authorizing the Secretary of the Interior To Withdraw and Reserve Lands of the Public Domain and Other Lands Owned or Controlled by the United States

flags.justification: This delegates presidential land-withdrawal authority to the Secretary of the Interior, but with multiple explicit safeguards: prior approval of the Director of the Bureau of the Budget AND the Attorney General is required for every order (treating Secretary-issued orders with the same procedural rigor as presidential EOs under EO 7298), plus Federal Register filing. One of the more carefully checked delegations in this corpus.
The Bureau of the Budget/Attorney General approval requirement and inter-agency concurrence requirement clearly establish review responsibility for every order issued under this delegation.
Single purpose: delegating land-withdrawal authority with specified safeguards.
This is a pure administrative delegation with safeguards, not a compliance regime against third parties.
No compliance regime exists for an incentive structure to attach to.

Standing-procedural-framework category applies: Sec. 2 shows this order itself supersedes a prior delegation order (EO 9146, issued exactly one year earlier), demonstrating the delegation is revised through ordinary supersession as needed rather than left static and unreviewed.
The order affirmatively adds oversight (Bureau of the Budget and Attorney General review) to what would otherwise be an unchecked delegation, rather than removing any existing check.
This is an inter-governmental delegation, not an ongoing regulated-party compliance regime.
A well-established administrative delegation pattern, evidenced by its own predecessor order; not a novel or concerning authority claim.

The second proviso explicitly requires 'prior concurrence of the head of the department or agency concerned' before any withdrawal affecting another agency's lands — an explicit, built-in anti-conflict safeguard.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.

notes: Validation batch (positive). Structural weight: 0/14 = 0.0%. A genuinely well-designed delegation order — the Bureau of the Budget/Attorney General approval requirement and inter-agency concurrence clause are real, substantive safeguards rarely seen with this level of specificity elsewhere in the corpus.
